diagrams.title: NIST Cloud Computing Reference Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows the five major actors in cloud computing and their interactions: Cloud Consumer, Cloud Provider, Cloud Carrier, Cloud Auditor, and Cloud Broker, along with the service categories (SaaS, PaaS, IaaS) and deployment models.
diagrams.purpose: Provides a high-level overview of the NIST cloud computing ecosystem, showing how different stakeholders interact and what services are available

diagrams.title: Cloud Service Models (SPI Model)
diagrams.description: Illustrates the three main cloud service models stacked vertically: Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS), Platform as a Service (PaaS), and Software as a Service (SaaS), showing the division of responsibilities between provider and consumer at each layer.
elements: SaaS layer (applications, data, runtime, middleware, O/S, virtualization, servers, storage, networking)
PaaS layer (runtime, middleware, O/S, virtualization, servers, storage, networking)
diagrams.purpose: Explains the difference between IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS and clarifies what management responsibilities fall to the provider vs. the consumer

diagrams.title: Cloud Deployment Models
diagrams.description: Visual representation of the four cloud deployment models: Public Cloud (shared resources, available to general public), Private Cloud (dedicated to single organization), Community Cloud (shared by specific community), and Hybrid Cloud (combination of two or more models).
elements: Public Cloud icon (multiple organizations, open access)
Private Cloud icon (single organization, dedicated infrastructure)
Community Cloud icon (shared by specific group with common concerns)
diagrams.purpose: Helps understand when and why different deployment models are chosen based on security, compliance, cost, and organizational needs
